content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the car positioned on the left side of the track, and the dashed center line is visible curving rightward — the car needs to continue steering right to follow the curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 105, target = (105 + 5) % 120 = 110
- Waypoint 110: x=1.5721, y=0.7864
- Car: x=1.4097, y=1.2309
- Bearing = atan2(0.7864 - 1.2309, 1.5721 - 1.4097) = atan2(-0.4445, 0.1624) ≈ -69.9°
- Current heading: -42.37°
- Delta = -69.9° - (-42.37°) = -27.5° → steer right

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves right ahead with car on left side; delta of -27.5° indicates a right turn needed, so steering -15 at speed 1.5 is appropriate for this moderate curve.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track loops sharply south around waypoints 103-115 then curves back northeast",
    "Waypoints 108-115 trend south/southeast — car must steer right significantly here",
    "Car heading ~-42° needs to decrease further (more negative) to follow track south",
    "Positive delta = steer left, negative delta = steer right",
    "At delta -25 to -35°, use steering_angle=-15 at speed 1.5",
    "Car is left of center — track curving right confirms right steering needed",
    "After waypoint 115, track begins heading northeast — watch for left turn",
    "Avoid oversteer oscillation on straights; use smallest valid angle when delta < 10°",
    "Track width ~0.76m, car is 0.20m left of center — within safe bounds",
    "Previous -20 steering at delta -38.6° was appropriate; now delta reduced to -27.5°"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.304, 1.369, 1.410],
    "y": [1.328, 1.273, 1.231],
    "yaw": [-42.0, -41.7, -42.4]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -27.5,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": -15.0
}
```
